sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Royale Union Saint-Gilloise are in exceptional form, unbeaten in their last 6 matches with 4 wins, including a dominant 4-1 victory recently. Standard Liège, however, have struggled, losing 3 of their last 5 matches, with only 1 win. This stark contrast highlights Union's momentum going into this clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Royale Union Saint-Gilloise have dominated Standard Liège, winning both matches 3-0. Union have not lost to Liège in their last 5 head-to-heads, with 4 wins and 1 draw, showcasing their clear edge in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Union lead the league standings with 52 points and a strong goal difference (+27), boasting the best defense (13 goals conceded). Standard Liège, sitting 9th with 30 points, have struggled offensively, scoring only 20 goals this season, which ranks them 15th in the league.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Union's Promise David (9 goals) and Raul Florucz (7 goals) are major threats upfront, while Standard's attack relies on Rafiki Saïd (4 goals). Union's missing key forward Kevin Rodriguez (7 goals) could impact their depth, but their squad remains potent.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Union's ability to score late (38.5% of goals after the 76th minute) could test Standard's defensive resilience, as Liège often concede first (4/5 matches). Standard's high corner count (4.33 per game) might offer set-piece opportunities, but Union's defensive solidity may neutralize this.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Union's superior form, head-to-head dominance, and league standing, they are clear favorites with a 58% win probability per Sofascore. Standard's inconsistent performances and offensive struggles make an upset unlikely. Expect Union to control the game and secure a victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Standard Liège holds a narrow lead over Royale Union Saint-Gilloise with a scoreline of 1-0. The home side managed to capitalize early through a well-taken goal by Dennis Eckert Ayensa in the 15th minute. Despite this, the away team has dominated possession and shown greater intent in the attacking third. The first half was characterized by contrasting styles, with Standard Liège focusing on defensive solidity and counter-attacks, while Union Saint-Gilloise controlled the tempo but failed to convert their chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance
halftimeSections.text: Standard Liège has been clinical despite being under pressure for much of the half. Their defense has held firm, though they’ve struggled to maintain possession and create consistent threats. On the other hand, Royale Union Saint-Gilloise has been the more dominant side, enjoying a significant edge in possession and creating more dangerous opportunities. However, their finishing has let them down, with several big chances missed. Their higher expected goals reflect their attacking potential, but they need to be more ruthless in front of goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half largely favored Royale Union Saint-Gilloise, especially in the opening stages and towards the end of the half, where they sustained prolonged attacking spells. Standard Liège had a notable shift in momentum around the time of their goal and maintained some pressure in the middle of the half with consistent attacking intent. However, Union Saint-Gilloise regained control in the latter minutes, pushing hard for an equalizer with relentless waves of attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: For Standard Liège, Dennis Eckert Ayensa has been the standout performer, not only for his goal but for his presence in the final third. Gustav Mortensen also impressed with his ability to create opportunities. On the away side, Anan Khalaili has been a constant threat, creating multiple chances for his teammates. Louis Patris also showed promise with his involvement in attack, though he missed a key opportunity to level the score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Standard Liège appears content to sit back and absorb pressure, looking to exploit spaces on the counter. Their defensive organization will need to remain tight as Union Saint-Gilloise is likely to push harder in the second half. The away team’s high possession and frequent entries into the penalty area suggest they might benefit from tactical adjustments to improve their finishing or vary their approach in the final third. Their superior duel-winning stats also indicate physical dominance that could wear down the home defense over time.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Royale Union Saint-Gilloise continue their aggressive approach as they chase an equalizer. Their higher expected goals and missed chances suggest they have the potential to turn the game around if they can find their clinical edge. Standard Liège, meanwhile, will aim to protect their lead and could look to exploit any overcommitment from the away side on the counter. The game remains finely balanced, but Union Saint-Gilloise seems poised to create more scoring opportunities.
